limitations: Depends on the local OpenCode CLI and its configured provider credentials being healthy.
Not the default owner-plane mutation lane for privileged repo work.
Reply structure can vary by provider/model and is normalized from CLI output.
mistakes: Do not assume OpenCode is free just because it is local; it still consumes configured provider quota.
Do not route privileged or owner-plane writes here when Local Codex is the safer lane.

limitations: Local runtime only; not ideal for cloud-heavy external research bursts.
Direct runs can consume local provider quota or take longer on bigger tasks.
mistakes: Do not default local Codex to broad external chores when a cheaper bounded worker would do.
Do not send owner-plane tasks to lower-trust external workers just because they look faster.

limitations: This lane is review-first and should not be treated as the owner-plane mutation path.
It depends on the authenticated Claude Code subscription path being healthy on this machine.
mistakes: Do not send owner-plane secrets or broad uncontrolled context into the Claude lane.
Do not offload final repo ownership decisions to Claude when Asolaria or local Codex should decide.

limitations: CLI is not yet a reliable unattended headless worker on this machine.
Cloud-heavy or deep-agent work should be treated as scarce and usage-aware.
mistakes: CLI print mode is still not reliable on this machine, so Abacus is not yet a safe fully headless worker.
The desktop app exposes many bundled VS Code-style surfaces and extensions; not every prompt or pane is relevant to Asolaria.
Listener is installed but not currently running, so listener-specific work should be treated as opportunistic.
Browser or deep-agent cloud work can consume subscription usage faster than desktop-local packet work.
Abacus outputs should be imported back through Asolaria packets rather than treated as direct ground truth.

limitations: Requires valid Linear-backed Symphony configuration and a healthy running service for pickup.
Depends on valid Symphony configuration and healthy Linear-backed setup.
mistakes: Do not route urgent single-shot chores into Symphony when a direct local or Abacus lane is available.
Do not assume issue creation means immediate execution; Symphony still decides pickup from the project queue.

engines.title: OpenMythos recurrent-depth cognitive engine
engines.description: Gated adapter contract for the OpenMythos PyTorch recurrent-depth transformer package.

engines.title: GA-SVM classification cognitive engine
engines.description: Gated adapter contract for genetic-algorithm optimized SVM classification tasks such as crop disease diagnosis.

engines.title: OpenMythos + GA-SVM fusion lane
engines.description: GA-SVM handles validated feature classification; OpenMythos reviews deep reasoning, drift, and explanation under GAC.

selectionRules.trigger: classification, crop disease, image/sensor features, SVM
deep reasoning, recurrent loops, high complexity
classification plus deep reasoning or explanation
